title: Refund Email
scenario: An email from your airline says your flight was cancelled. It offers an instant refund if you click a link and confirm your bank details immediately.

answers.A: Click to get your money back fast.
answers.C: Check your booking on the official app or website.
answers.D: Call the airline using the number on their official website.

explanation: Airline refund messages should be verified through official booking channels. Scammers use refund urgency to make you click links and share bank details.
proTip: Never use links or phone numbers from the message. Use the official app, website, or known contact channel.
